message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Phase A: Tactile UI + Chat/Notifications (на моках)

## Context
Текущий UI функциональный, но "AI-шаблонный" — плоский, без тактильного отклика, без звуков. Вдохновение: Remilia.net — каждое действие имеет визуальный и звуковой фидбек, кнопки ощущаются физическими. Цель: сделать интерфейс живым, добавить sidebar с уведомлениями и чатом (на моках, без бэкенда).

## Порядок: A1 → A2 → A3 → A4 → A5 (каждый шаг — рабочий билд)

---

## A1: Tactile Interaction System (9 файлов)

**Цель:** Spring-анимации везде, тактильные кнопки, grain-текстура.

| Файл | Изменения |
|------|-----------|
| `src/styles/_variables.scss` | Добавить SCSS-переменные: `$pressScale`, `$shadowInsetPress` и т.д. |
| `src/components/Button/Button.tsx` | `whileTap={{ scale: 0.95, y: 2 }}`, spring transition (stiffness: 400, damping: 25) |
| `src/components/Button/Button.module.scss` | `:active` — inset box-shadow, fix `.ghost` rgba → `var(--glass-bg-hover)` |
| `src/components/CandidateCard/CandidateCard.tsx` | Добавить `whileTap={{ scale: 0.98 }}`, spring hover |
| `src/components/PageTransition/PageTransition.tsx` | `ease: "easeInOut"` → `type: "spring", stiffness: 300, damping: 30` |
| `src/pages/Landing/Landing.tsx` | Все duration/ease → spring transitions |
| `src/pages/Search/Search.tsx` | `cardVariants` и заголовки → spring |
| `src/pages/CandidateProfile/CandidateProfile.tsx` | videoSection/info → spring |
| `src/styles/global.scss` | `body::after` — SVG noise texture (opacity: 0.03, fixed, pointer-events: none) |

---

## A2: Sound System (7 файлов, 3 новых)

**Цель:** Синтезированные звуки через Web Audio API (без mp3, без зависимостей). Opt-in toggle.

| Файл | Тип | Описание |
|------|-----|----------|
| `src/contexts/SoundContext.tsx` | **new** | `SoundProvider` + `useSound()`. Web Audio oscillator synthesis. 6 звуков: click, hover, success, error, notify, navigate. localStorage toggle. |
| `src/hooks/useSound.ts` | **new** | Re-export из SoundContext для единообразия с useApi/useSearch |
| `src/assets/sounds/README.md` | **new** | Placeholder — можно позже заменить синтез на mp3 |
| `src/main.tsx` | mod | Обернуть в `<SoundProvider>` |
| `src/components/Button/Button.tsx` | mod | `playSound("click")` при клике |
| `src/components/Navbar/Navbar.tsx` | mod | `playSound("navigate")` при переходах, кнопка toggle звука (speaker icon) |
| `src/components/Navbar/Navbar.module.scss` | mod | Стили `.soundToggle` |

---

## A3: Layout — Command Center (7 файлов, 3 новых)

**Цель:** Правый sidebar (280px) с секциями для уведомлений и чата. На мобиле — drawer.

| Файл | Тип | Описание |
|------|-----|----------|
| `src/contexts/SidebarContext.tsx` | **new** | `SidebarProvider` + `useSidebar()`. Управление open/close для мобильного drawer. |
| `src/components/Sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` | **new** | Desktop: sticky aside. Mobile: AnimatePresence overlay + slide-in drawer. Две секции: notifications (top, 45% max) + chat (bottom, flex: 1). |
| `src/components/Sidebar/Sidebar.module.scss` | **new** | `.sidebar` (desktop), `.overlay` + `.drawer` (mobile @include tablet) |
| `src/components/Layout/Layout.tsx` | mod | Добавить `<Sidebar />`, новая структура: `.layout > Navbar + .body > (.main + Sidebar)` |
| `src/components/Layout/Layout.module.scss` | mod | `.body { display: flex }`, `.main { flex: 1; min-width: 0 }` |
| `src/main.tsx` | mod | Добавить `<SidebarProvider>` |
| `src/components/Navbar/Navbar.tsx` | mod | Кнопка sidebar toggle (visible only @tablet) |

---

## A4: Notifications (8 файлов, 5 новых)

**Цель:** Панель уведомлений с мок-данными, имитация real-time через setTimeout.

| Файл | Тип | Описание |
|------|-----|----------|
| `src/data/mockNotifications.ts` | **new** | Типы: new_candidate, interview_scheduled, message_received, status_change. 4 начальных + 2 incoming (через 15/30 сек). |
| `src/contexts/NotificationContext.tsx` | **new** | `NotificationProvider` + `useNotifications()`. State: notifications[], unreadCount, dismiss(), markRead(), markAllRead(). setTimeout для incoming. |
| `src/components/NotificationPanel/NotificationPanel.tsx + .module.scss` | **new** | Список с AnimatePresence (spring enter/exit). Header: "N new" + "Mark all read". |
| `src/components/NotificationItem/NotificationItem.tsx + .module.scss` | **new** | Иконка по типу, message, timeAgo, action button + dismiss. Unread стиль: accent border-left + tinted bg. |
| `src/main.tsx` | mod | Добавить `<NotificationProvider>` |
| `src/components/Sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` | mod | Заменить placeholder на `<NotificationPanel />` |
| `src/components/Navbar/Navbar.tsx` | mod | Bell icon + badge с unreadCount |
| `src/components/Navbar/Navbar.module.scss` | mod | `.notificationBell`, `.badge` стили |

---

## A5: Chat Panel (11 файлов, 8 новых)

**Цель:** Чат в нижней части sidebar. Список диалогов ↔ открытый диалог. Интеграция с CandidateProfile.

| Файл | Тип | Описание |
|------|-----|----------|
| `src/data/mockConversations.ts` | **new** | 3 диалога с реалистичными сообщениями (recruiter ↔ candidate) |
| `src/contexts/ChatContext.tsx` | **new** | `ChatProvider` + `useChat()`. State: conversations[], activeConversationId, sendMessage(), startConversation(), openConversationByCandidate(). |
| `src/components/ChatPanel/ChatPanel.tsx + .module.scss` | **new** | Два состояния: ConversationList или active conversation (header + messages + input). AnimatePresence slide transition. |
| `src/components/ChatMessage/ChatMessage.tsx + .module.scss` | **new** | Bubble: mine (accent tint, right-aligned) / theirs (glass bg, left-aligned). Timestamp. |
| `src/components/ChatInput/ChatInput.tsx + .module.scss` | **new** | Input + send button (spring whileTap). Enter to send. playSound("click"). |
| `src/components/ConversationList/ConversationList.tsx + .module.scss` | **new** | Avatar (initial letter), name, last message preview, timeAgo, unread badge. |
| `src/main.tsx` | mod | Добавить `<ChatProvider>` |
| `src/components/Sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` | mod | Заменить chat placeholder на `<ChatPanel />` |
| `src/pages/CandidateProfile/CandidateProfile.tsx` | mod | "Contact Candidate" → opens/creates conversation via useChat() |

---

## Финальное дерево провайдеров (main.tsx)

```
StrictMode
  ThemeProvider
    SoundProvider
      SidebarProvider
        NotificationProvider
          ChatProvider
            RouterProvider
```

## Итого файлов

| Phase | Изменённых | Новых | Всего |
|-------|-----------|-------|-------|
| A1 | 9 | 0 | 9 |
| A2 | 4 | 3 | 7 |
| A3 | 4 | 3 | 7 |
| A4 | 3 | 5 | 8 |
| A5 | 3 | 8 | 11 |
| **Итого** | **~17 уникальных** | **19** | **~36** |

## Ключевые решения

1. **Web Audio API** вместо mp3 — нет зависимостей, нет файлов. Можно заменить на реальные звуки позже.
2. **Sidebar всегда в DOM** на desktop (CSS hide на mobile) — не теряет state при resize.
3. **Моки в src/data/** — при подключении бэкенда меняются только контексты, компоненты не трогаем.
4. **Spring configs inline** — каждый компонент тюнит stiffness/damping под свой "вес". Не централизуем.

## Проверка после каждого шага

1. `npm run check-types` — без ошибок
2. `npm run build` — собирается
3. `npm run dev` — визуальная проверка
4. После A5: переключить тему → все новые компоненты корректно перекрашиваются


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\yakik\.claude\projects\C--Users-yakik-Documents-projects-rearden\63f5ab8e-2767-479c-bb13-733777698d49.jsonl

1→# Rearden Project Memory
     2→
     3→## Architecture
     4→- Turborepo monorepo: `apps/web` (Vite+React), `apps/api` (Hono), `packages/types`, `packages/tsconfig`
     5→- Scope: `@rearden/*`
     6→- Ports: web=3000, api=3001
     7→
     8→## Key Gotchas
     9→- **Prisma 7**: `datasource.url` removed from schema.prisma — must use `prisma.config.ts` with `defineConfig()` from `prisma/config`. See [prisma-notes.md](prisma-notes.md).
    10→- **Types package**: exports raw `.ts` source (no build step), uses `"exports": { ".": "./src/index.ts" }`

content.content:
1→# Prisma 7 Notes
     2→
     3→## Breaking Change: No `url` in schema datasource
     4→Prisma 7 removed `url = env("DATABASE_URL")` from `schema.prisma`. Instead:
     5→
     6→1. Schema only declares provider:
     7→```prisma
     8→datasource db {
     9→  provider = "sqlite"
    10→}
    11→```
    12→
    13→2. Connection config goes in `prisma.config.ts`:
    14→```ts
    15→import "dotenv/config";
    16→import { defineConfig, env } from "prisma/config";
    17→
    18→export default defineConfig({
    19→  schema: "prisma/schema.prisma",
    20→  migrations: { path: "prisma/migrations" },
    21→  datasource: { url: env("DATABASE_URL") },
    22→});
    23→```
    24→
    25→## Generator
    26→- Use `prisma-client` (not `prisma-client-js`) for Prisma 7
    27→- Output to custom dir: `output = "../src/generated/prisma"`
    28→

- Turborepo monorepo structure: `apps/web` (Vite+React), `apps/api` (Hono), `packages/types`, `packages/tsconfig`

**Key Gotchas:**

3. **Motion v12**: Import from `"motion/react"`, not `"framer-motion"`

---

**Prisma 7 Breaking Changes:**
- `url = env("DATABASE_URL")` removed from `schema.prisma`
- Schema only declares provider (provider = "sqlite")
- Connection config moved to `prisma.config.ts` using `defineConfig()` from `prisma/config`
- Example config includes: schema path, migrations path, datasource url

**Generator Settings:**
- Use `prisma-client` (not `prisma-client-js`) for Prisma 7
- Output to custom directory: `output = "../src/generated/prisma"`

---

### 2. Key Files - Full Contents

#### **apps/web/src/main.tsx**
Entry point that wraps app with ThemeProvider and RouterProvider.

#### **apps/web/src/styles/_variables.scss**
Contains SCSS variables for colors, spacing, radii, shadows, typography, breakpoints, and layout constants. Uses CSS custom properties from _tokens.scss.

**Key values:**
- Colors: bgDeep, bgBase, bgElevated, bgSurface, textPrimary/Secondary/Muted
- Accents: accentPurple/Light/Dark, accentTeal/Light
- Spacing: $space1 through $space20 (4px to 80px)
- Radii: $radiusSm (6px) to $radiusFull (9999px)
- Typography: $fontSize12 through $fontSize64
- Breakpoints: $bpMobile (480px), $bpTablet (768px), $bpDesktop (1024px), $bpWide (1280px)

#### **apps/web/src/styles/global.scss**
CSS resets and global styles:
- Box-sizing, font smoothing, default font family
- Removes default margins/padding from buttons, inputs
- Configures focus-visible with purple outline
- Sets body background to dark theme

#### **apps/web/src/components/Button/Button.tsx**
Flexible Button component with:
- Variants: "primary" (gradient), "secondary" (glass), "ghost" (transparent)
- Sizes: "sm" (32px), "md" (40px), "lg" (48px)
- Can render as `<button>` or `<a>` (if `href` prop provided)
- Uses Motion v12 for scale animations on hover/tap
- Disabled state support

#### **apps/web/src/components/Button/Button.module.scss**
Button styles:
- `.primary`: Gradient from accentPurple to accentPurpleLight, glow on hover
- `.secondary`: Glass morphism with accentPurple border
- `.ghost`: Transparent with subtle white hover state
- Focus ring included via mixin

#### **apps/web/src/components/CandidateCard/CandidateCard.tsx**
Displays candidate info in card format:
- Shows thumbnail image (or placeholder)
- Displays score badge if provided
- Name, title, location
- Max 4 skills displayed
- Optional match reason
- Hover animation lifts card slightly (y: -4)
- Clickable with navigation

#### **apps/web/src/components/PageTransition/PageTransition.tsx**
Wraps page content with fade-in/out animations:
- Initial: opacity 0, y: 20
- Animate: opacity 1, y: 0
- Exit: opacity 0, y: -20
- Duration: 0.3s

#### **apps/web/src/components/Layout/Layout.tsx**
Main layout wrapper:
- Fixed Navbar at top
- Main content area below navbar
- Uses AnimatePresence from Motion for page transitions
- PageTransition wrapper with location-based key for route changes

#### **apps/web/src/components/Layout/Layout.module.scss**
Simple flex layout with navbar height padding on main content.

#### **apps/web/src/components/Navbar/Navbar.tsx**
Navigation bar with:
- Logo link to home
- Nav links to Search and Register
- Active link indicator (animated underline)
- Theme selector (Select component)
- Uses NavLink with render prop pattern for active state

#### **apps/web/src/components/Navbar/Navbar.module.scss**
Fixed navbar with glass morphism effect, spring animation for active indicator.

#### **apps/web/src/pages/Landing/Landing.tsx**
Hero landing page with:
- Animated badge "The future of hiring"
- H1 with gradient text and strikethrough "resumes"
- Two CTAs: Start Searching, Join as Candidate
- Features section (3 cards): Video-First, AI-Powered, Lightning Fast
- Stats section: 500+ profiles, 85% faster, 3x match rate
- CTA section with logo and Explore button
- Extensive use of Motion staggering animations

#### **apps/web/src/pages/Search/Search.tsx**
Search results page:
- SearchBar component (with typing)
- Grid of CandidateCard components
- Shows results count
- Loading skeleton (6 cards)
- Empty state with icon if no results
- Staggered card animations
- Click card to navigate to profile

#### **apps/web/src/pages/CandidateProfile/CandidateProfile.tsx**
Individual candidate profile:
- Fetches candidate data via useApi
- Video player (with poster image)
- Name, title, location, experience, availability
- About section with bio
- Skills display
- Contact button and back button
- Availability labels mapped from enum values

#### **apps/web/src/styles/_tokens.scss**
CSS custom properties for **10 themes**:
1. **Y2K** (default): Lime green (#c8f000) + teal accents
2. **Purple Night**: Deep purple (#6c5ce7) + teal
3. **Rearden Metal**: Teal (#7ec8b8) + desaturated palette
4. **Steel Frost** (light): Teal + light backgrounds
5. **Forge**: Brown/orange (#b05a1a) + gold accents
6. **Neon Y2K**: Hot pink (#ff2d8a) + lime
7. **Daylight** (light): Orange (#ff5c1a) + teal
8. **Bliss** (light): Green (#0d7a62) + blue
9. **Aurora**: Dark blue + cyan/green
10. **Meadow** (light): Teal + green

Each theme defines: colors, glass properties, shadows with theme-specific glows.

#### **apps/web/src/styles/_animations.scss**
Keyframe animations:
- `shimmer`: Loading effect
- `pulse`: Opacity fade
- `glow`: Shadow pulsing
- `glowBorder`: Border color animation
- `spin`: Full rotation
- `gradientShift`: Background position shift

#### **apps/web/src/styles/_mixins.scss**
Reusable SCSS mixins:
- **Media queries**: @mixin mobile, tablet, tabletUp, desktop, wide
- **Glass morphism**: @mixin glass, @mixin glassHover
- **Container**: Centered max-width with responsive padding
- **Truncate**: Line clamping (1 line or multi-line)
- **Focus ring**: Purple outline
- **Custom scrollbar**: Styled webkit scrollbar

#### **apps/web/src/contexts/ThemeContext.tsx**
Theme management context:
- Type: `Theme` union of 10 theme values
- Provider wraps app, stores theme in localStorage
- `useTheme()` hook returns {theme, setTheme}
- Default theme: "y2k"
- Sets `data-theme` attribute on document root

#### **apps/web/src/hooks/useApi.ts**
Generic API hook:
- Takes path string
- Returns {data, loading, error, refetch}
- Fetches on mount
- Uses apiFetch under the hood

#### **apps/web/src/hooks/useSearch.ts**
Search-specific hook:
- Debounced search (400ms)
- Two API paths: `/candidates` (if empty) or `/search` (POST)
- Returns SearchResultWithCandidate[] with candidateId, score, matchReason, candidate
- Handles loading/error states

#### **apps/web/src/lib/api.ts**
Minimal API client:
- `apiFetch<T>(path, options?)` - wrapper around fetch
- Base URL: `/api`
- Sets Content-Type header
- Throws on non-ok responses

#### **apps/web/src/components/SearchBar/SearchBar.tsx**
Search input component:
- Cycling placeholders (4 options, rotates every 3s)
- Loading spinner icon
- Optional onSubmit callback
- Styled input with search icon
- Debouncing handled by parent (useSearch)

#### **apps/web/src/components/VideoPlayer/VideoPlayer.tsx**
Custom video player:
- Play button overlay
- Click toggles play
- Native controls appear when playing
- Handles onPlay, onPause, onEnded events
- Poster image support

---

### 3. Architecture Highlights

**Component Structure:**
- All components follow the pattern: Component.tsx + Component.module.scss
- SCSS Modules with camelCase class names
- Motion v12 animations for interactive elements

**Styling System:**
- CSS custom properties (tokens) for theme switching
- SCSS variables bridge to tokens
- Mixins for common patterns (glass, container, truncate, focus ring)
- 10 complete color themes with light/dark variants

**State Management:**
- Context API (ThemeContext) for theme
- React hooks (useState, useEffect, useCallback) for local state
- Custom hooks for API and search logic

**Routing:**
- React Router v6 with nested routes
- 4 main pages: Landing, Search, CandidateProfile, Register
- Layout wrapper with AnimatePresence for transitions

**API Layer:**
- Minimal fetch wrapper
- `useApi` for GET requests
- `useSearch` for search with debouncing
- Both expect `/api` base path

---

### 4. Key Dependencies Inferred
- React 19
- React Router v6
- Motion v12 (for animations)
- SCSS Modules
- @rearden/types (shared types)

---

This is a well-structured video-first hiring platform frontend with strong attention to theming, animations, and user experience patterns. The modular component design, consistent styling system, and clear separation of concerns make it maintainable and extensible.

content.thinking:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the entire codebase. Let me create task tracking and start implementing all 5 phases. I'll work through them systematically.

Let me plan the implementation:

**A1: Tactile Interaction System** (9 file modifications)
1. `_variables.scss` - Add $pressScale, $shadowInsetPress
2. `Button.tsx` - whileTap scale 0.95 + y:2, spring transition
3. `Button.module.scss` - :active inset box-shadow, fix .ghost rgba
4. `CandidateCard.tsx` - whileTap scale 0.98, spring hover


5. `PageTransition.tsx` - spring instead of easeInOut
6. `Landing.tsx` - All duration/ease → spring
7. `Search.tsx` - cardVariants + headers → spring
8. `CandidateProfile.tsx` - videoSection/info → spring
9. `global.scss` - body::after grain texture

**A2: Sound System** (7 files, 3 new)
1. NEW: `SoundContext.tsx` - Web Audio oscillator synthesis
2. NEW: `useSound.ts` - Re-export
3. NEW: `sounds/README.md` - Placeholder
4. MOD: `main.tsx` - Wrap in SoundProvider
5. MOD: `Button.tsx` - playSound("click")
6. MOD: `Navbar.tsx` - playSound("navigate"), toggle button
7. MOD: `Navbar.module.scss` - .soundToggle styles

**A3: Layout — Command Center** (7 files, 3 new)
1. NEW: `SidebarContext.tsx`
2. NEW: `Sidebar.tsx`
3. NEW: `Sidebar.module.scss`
4. MOD: `Layout.tsx` - Add Sidebar
5. MOD: `Layout.module.scss` - Flex layout
6. MOD: `main.tsx` - SidebarProvider
7. MOD: `Navbar.tsx` - Sidebar toggle button

**A4: Notifications** (8 files, 5 new)
1. NEW: `mock Notifications.ts`
2. NEW: `NotificationContext.tsx`
3. NEW: `NotificationPanel.tsx + .module.scss`
4. NEW: `NotificationItem.tsx + .module.scss`
5. MOD: `main.tsx` - NotificationProvider
6. MOD: `Sidebar.tsx` - NotificationPanel
7. MOD: `Navbar.tsx` - Bell icon + badge
8. MOD: `Navbar.module.scss` - .notificationBell, .badge

**A5: Chat Panel** (11 files, 8 new)
1. NEW: `mockConversations.ts`
2. NEW: `ChatContext.tsx`
3. NEW: `ChatPanel.tsx + .module.scss`
4. NEW: `ChatMessage.tsx + .module.scss`
5. NEW: `ChatInput.tsx + .module.scss`
6. NEW: `ChatWindow.tsx + .module.scss`
7. MOD: `main.tsx` - ChatProvider
8. MOD: `Sidebar.tsx` - ChatPanel
9. MOD: `Navbar.tsx` - Chat icon
10. MOD: `Navbar.module.scss` - .chatIcon
11. MOD: `Layout.module.scss` - Chat panel spacing

This is a substantial implementation. I'm going to tackle A1 first since I have everything ready, then work through the rest in parallel where I can. Starting with the tactile design variables and button interactions—I'm adding press scale and inset shadow effects, then updating the Button component to use spring animations on hover and tap states.

Adding tactile feedback styles... Still writing transition configs...

Now I'm mapping out the implementation order—I'll tackle all the A1 changes across the codebase first, then move through A2 and A3 phases systematically to keep things organized.

content.text: Build succeeds. Let me mark A5 complete.

content.content: Updated task #5 status

content.text: All 5 phases are complete. Let me verify the final state of key files to ensure everything is correct.

Provider tree matches the spec. Here's a summary of everything implemented:

## Phase A Complete — All 5 Steps

### A1: Tactile Interaction System (9 files)
- Added `$pressScale`, `$shadowInsetPress` SCSS variables
- Button: `whileTap: { scale: 0.95, y: 2 }` with spring (400/25), `:active` inset shadow
- CandidateCard: `whileTap: { scale: 0.98 }` with spring
- PageTransition: spring (300/30) replacing `easeInOut`
- Landing/Search/CandidateProfile: all `duration/ease` → spring transitions
- `.ghost` button: `$glassBgHover` replacing hardcoded rgba
- `body::after`: SVG noise grain texture (opacity: 0.03, fixed)

### A2: Sound System (7 files, 3 new)
- `SoundContext.tsx`: Web Audio oscillator synthesis, 6 sounds (click/hover/success/error/notify/navigate), localStorage opt-in toggle
- `useSound.ts`: re-export hook
- Button plays `click` sound, Navbar plays `navigate` on route change
- Speaker icon toggle in Navbar

### A3: Layout — Command Center (7 files, 3 new)
- `SidebarContext.tsx`: open/close/toggle state
- `Sidebar.tsx`: desktop sticky aside + mobile drawer with `AnimatePresence`
- `Layout.tsx`: new flex structure (`.body > .main + Sidebar`)
- Sidebar toggle button in Navbar (visible only at `@tablet`)

### A4: Notifications (8 files, 5 new)
- `mockNotifications.ts`: 4 initial + 2 incoming (15s/30s setTimeout)
- `NotificationContext.tsx`: state, unread count, dismiss/markRead/markAllRead
- `NotificationPanel.tsx` + `NotificationItem.tsx`: animated list with spring enter/exit
- Bell icon + unread badge in Navbar

### A5: Chat Panel (11 files, 8 new)
- `mockConversations.ts`: 3 realistic recruiter↔candidate dialogs
- `ChatContext.tsx`: conversations, openConversation, sendMessage, startConversation
- `ChatPanel.tsx`: conversation list ↔ active conversation with slide transitions
- `ChatMessage.tsx`: mine (accent-tinted, right) / theirs (glass, left) bubbles
- `ChatInput.tsx`: input + send button with spring tap, Enter to send, plays click sound
- `ConversationList.tsx`: avatar initials, last message preview, timeAgo, unread badges
- CandidateProfile "Contact Candidate" → `startConversation()`

### Provider tree (main.tsx)
```
StrictMode → ThemeProvider → SoundProvider → SidebarProvider → NotificationProvider → ChatProvider → RouterProvider
```

### Verification
- `npm run check-types` — 0 errors
- `npm run build` — succeeds (455 KB JS, 41 KB CSS)

message.content: Сделай пожалуйста отдельную вкладку чата, чтобы можно было перейти на отдельную страницу. Справишься? Чтобы все было сконнекчено с бэком и сообщения приходили в лайве, можно было закрепить чаты, у чатов были вкладки, короче все как в телеграме, у тг самый удобный мессенджер. Но дизайн как у ремилии, получится такое сдеать?

content.thinking:
The user wants a full-featured chat page (like Telegram) with:
1. Separate chat page/route (not just sidebar)
2. Connected to backend with live messages
3. Pin chats
4. Chat tabs/folders
5. Telegram-like UX but Remilia-style design (tactile, spring animations, grain texture)

This is a significant feature. Let me plan this properly since it involves:
- New route/page for chat
- Backend API endpoints for chat (Hono)
- WebSocket or SSE for live messages
- Chat pinning


- Organizing chats with tabs and folders, all styled with Remilia's tactile aesthetic and spring animations

I need to think through the architecture here since there are multiple moving parts and design decisions to make.

content.text: Масштабная задача — отдельная страница чата с бэкендом, вебсокетами, пинами, вкладками. Давай спланирую.

1→export interface Message {
     2→  id: string;
     3→  senderId: string;
     4→  text: string;
     5→  timestamp: Date;
     6→}
     7→
     8→export interface Conversation {
     9→  id: string;
    10→  candidateId: string;
    11→  candidateName: string;
    12→  candidateInitial: string;
    13→  messages: Message[];
    14→  unread: number;
    15→}
    16→
    17→const ME = "recruiter";
    18→
    19→export const mockConversations: Conversation[] = [
    20→  {
    21→    id: "c1",
    22→    candidateId: "1",
    23→    candidateName: "Sarah Chen",
    24→    candidateInitial: "S",
    25→    unread: 1,
    26→    messages: [
    27→      {
    28→        id: "m1",
    29→        senderId: ME,
    30→        text: "Hi Sarah! I was really impressed by your video profile. Would you be interested in discussing a Senior React role?",
    31→        timestamp: new Date(Date.now() - 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24),
    32→      },
    33→      {
    34→        id: "m2",
    35→        senderId: "1",
    36→        text: "Thank you! I'd love to hear more about the role. What's the team like?",
    37→        timestamp: new Date(Date.now() - 1000 * 60 * 60 * 23),
    38→      },
    39→      {
    40→        id: "m3",
    41→        senderId: ME,
    42→        text: "It's a tight-knit team of 6 engineers working on our core product. Very collaborative culture with a focus on code quality.",
    43→        timestamp: new Date(Date.now() - 1000 * 60 * 60 * 22),
    44→      },
    45→      {
    46→        id: "m4",
    47→        senderId: "1",
    48→        text: "That sounds great! I'm definitely interested in learning more. When would be a good time for a quick call?",
    49→        timestamp: new Date(Date.now() - 1000 * 60 * 30),
    50→      },
    51→    ],
    52→  },
    53→  {
    54→    id: "c2",
    55→    candidateId: "2",
    56→    candidateName: "Alex Rivera",
    57→    candidateInitial: "A",
    58→    unread: 0,
    59→    messages: [
    60→      {
    61→        id: "m5",
    62→        senderId: ME,
    63→        text: "Alex, your full-stack experience looks amazing. We have an opening that might be perfect for you.",
    64→        timestamp: new Date(Date.now() - 1000 * 60 * 60 * 48),
    65→      },
    66→      {
    67→        id: "m6",
    68→        senderId: "2",
    69→        text: "Thanks for reaching out! I checked out the company and it looks like a great fit. Let's schedule something!",
    70→        timestamp: new Date(Date.now() - 1000 * 60 * 60 * 47),
    71→      },
    72→      {
    73→        id: "m7",
    74→        senderId: ME,
    75→        text: "Perfect. I've sent a calendar invite for tomorrow at 2 PM. Looking forward to it!",
    76→        timestamp: new Date(Date.now() - 1000 * 60 * 60 * 46),
    77→      },
    78→    ],
    79→  },
    80→  {
    81→    id: "c3",
    82→    candidateId: "3",
    83→    candidateName: "Jordan Lee",
    84→    candidateInitial: "J",
    85→    unread: 2,
    86→    messages: [
    87→      {
    88→        id: "m8",
    89→        senderId: "3",
    90→        text: "Hi! I saw the job posting and wanted to reach out directly. My video profile covers most of my recent work.",
    91→        timestamp: new Date(Date.now() - 1000 * 60 * 60 * 3),
    92→      },
    93→      {
    94→        id: "m9",
    95→        senderId: "3",
    96→        text: "I'm particularly excited about the AI/ML aspects of the role. I've been working with LLMs for the past year.",
    97→        timestamp: new Date(Date.now() - 1000 * 60 * 60 * 2),
    98→      },
    99→    ],
   100→  },

Now I have a thorough understanding of both frontend and backend. Let me design the plan for the full chat page with backend integration.

Key considerations:
1. Backend needs new models (Conversation, Message) in Prisma schema
2. WebSocket support needs to be added to Hono (or SSE which is simpler)
3. New API routes for chat CRUD operations
4. Frontend needs a full chat page (Telegram-like)
5. The sidebar chat should still work but perhaps link to the full page
6. Pin chats, tabs/folders

Let me ask some clarifying questions before finalizing the plan.

Actually, let me think about the architecture:


The user wants real backend integration with live messages, so I need to decide between keeping chat in-memory like the current candidates or actually persisting to the database. For real-time messaging, I'm weighing WebSocket via Hono against simpler alternatives like SSE or polling, with WebSocket being the most appropriate for a live chat experience. Telegram-like experience, WebSocket makes the most sense.

**Chat tabs/folders:**
Telegram has: All Chats, Unread, Personal, Groups, etc.
For our recruiting platform: All, Unread, Pinned, maybe by status (Active, Archived)

**Features needed:**
1. Full page at `/chat` with Telegram-like layout
2. Left panel: conversation list with tabs (All, Unread, Pinned)
3. Right panel: active conversation with messages
4. Pin/unpin conversations
5. Live messages via WebSocket
6. Backend API for chat CRUD
7. Prisma models for persistence

The user mentioned "дизайн как у ремилии" — Remilia's tactile, spring-animated, grain-textured aesthetic — so the chat interface should match those existing design patterns. I'm thinking the sidebar chat stays as a mini-view with an expand button, I'll use SQLite via Prisma for persistent storage, and WebSocket handles real-time updates. But I need to clarify a few things first, particularly around authentication and whether the sidebar should link to the full page or remain independent. simulated candidate auto-replies to show it working, and I'll persist messages to SQLite via Prisma. Now I'm thinking through the sidebar behavior—the user wants a Telegram-like experience, so I should build a full chat page at `/chat` with a two-panel layout: conversation list on the left with tabs for All, Unread, and Pinned conversations, plus search and pin/unpin functionality, and the active chat on the right with live WebSocket messages. For the database schema, I need to add Conversation and Message models to Prisma alongside the existing User model, then set up WebSocket handling with Hono's node-server support.

I'm realizing `@hono/node-server` doesn't natively support WebSocket upgrades, so I'll need to use the `@hono/node-ws` package instead. Actually, I could simplify this with Server-Sent Events for server-to-client push and regular POST requests for client-to-server, but since the user specifically wants live Telegram-like messaging, WebSocket is the better choice here.

Looking at what needs to happen: I need to add Conversation and Message models to Prisma, run migrations, seed some initial data, then build out REST endpoints for CRUD operations plus a WebSocket route for live messaging on the backend. The frontend will need a full chat interface with tabs and pinning functionality.

But wait—I'm checking the current setup and realizing the Prisma client is already generated but not actually being used anywhere. The candidates are stored in-memory in `mockCandidates.ts`, and there's no `.env` file set up yet, so I need to handle the database configuration first before adding new models.

Given that the user asked "справишься?" and provided specific requirements, they clearly want me to move forward with this. They also mentioned the design should match Remilia's aesthetic, which the existing interface already captures with the glass morphism and spring animations—I'll keep that consistent for the chat page.

Let me structure the full implementation plan and present it to them.

For the architecture, I'm thinking through the WebSocket approach more carefully. With Hono and the node server, I'd need to set up a WebSocket handler that manages client connections and broadcasts messages. Since this is a single recruiter scenario, simulating candidate replies makes sense—the WebSocket would accept connections, broadcast sent messages to all clients, and optionally add a typing indicator followed by an auto-reply after a delay to create that Telegram-like conversational feel.

Now I'm laying out the full implementation plan. The backend needs Prisma models for conversations and messages, API routes for CRUD operations, and a WebSocket endpoint for live messaging. On the frontend, I'm building a two-panel Telegram-style chat page at `/chat` with conversation tabs for filtering.

I'm reconsidering the WebSocket setup—I need to use `@hono/node-ws` to handle the upgrade properly...

Setting up WebSocket integration...

Now I'm defining the message protocol with types like `message:send`, `message:new`, `conversation:update`, and typing indicators so the client and server can communicate clearly.

Let me write out the full implementation plan now that I have the architecture sorted. справишься?"). I should be confident and clear. The plan should be comprehensive but practical.

Let me also consider: should the sidebar chat be removed or kept as a mini-view? I think keeping it as a "quick peek" that links to the full page makes sense. The sidebar would show recent conversations and clicking one would navigate to `/chat/conversationId`. Or it could open a mini-view for quick replies while maintaining the full page for detailed interactions.

I think the best UX is:
- Sidebar: shows conversation list as before, but clicking opens the chat page
- Full page at `/chat`: two-panel layout with all features
- `/chat/:conversationId` for direct links to specific conversations

This is like Telegram Web where you can have conversations in a sidebar OR navigate to the full chat page.

Now I'm thinking about what tabs would make sense for a recruiting platform. Instead of Telegram's folders, I should focus on what recruiters actually need: All, Unread, Pinned, and maybe Active conversations. I'll keep it simple with just All, Unread, and Pinned.

For the demo to feel more realistic, I can add auto-reply simulation where candidates respond after a 2-5 second delay with contextual messages. This would happen server-side through WebSocket, making the chat feel alive.

Now I'm ready to outline the implementation plan, starting with the backend foundation and working through the database schema updates. Message models)
- Create .env if needed with DATABASE_URL
- Run migration
- Seed initial data

### Phase 2: API Routes
- GET /api/chat/conversations - list conversations (with filters: all, unread, pinned)
- GET /api/chat/conversations/:id - get conversation with messages
- POST /api/chat/conversations - create conversation
- PATCH /api/chat/conversations/:id - update (pin/unpin, archive)
- POST /api/chat/conversations/:id/messages - send message
- DELETE /api/chat/conversations/:id - delete/archive

### Phase 3: WebSocket
- /ws/chat - live connection
- Message protocol: send, receive, typing indicators
- Auto-reply simulation

### Phase 4: Shared Types
- Add chat types to @rearden/types

### Phase 5: Frontend - Chat Page
- New page at /chat with route
- Two-panel layout (left: conversations, right: messages)
- Tabs for All, Unread, and Pinned conversations
- Pin/unpin functionality with Remilia-style design (glass, springs, grain)

### Phase 6: Frontend - Integration
- useWebSocket hook
- Update ChatContext to use backend
- Update sidebar to link to chat page
- Update navbar with chat nav link

Now I'm deciding between Prisma and in-memory storage. The current API uses in-memory data like the candidates endpoint, which is simpler but loses everything on restart. Prisma adds setup overhead with migrations and seeding, but since the user specifically asked for backend connection, I should go with a proper database solution. I'll go with in-memory storage on the backend to match the existing pattern—it works immediately without database setup, persists while the server runs, and gives me a clean API layer I can swap to Prisma later if needed. Now let me map out the concrete file changes I need to make.

For the backend, I'm adding a chat data store for in-memory storage, REST API routes, and a WebSocket handler, then wiring those into the main app. On the shared types side, I'm creating chat type definitions. The frontend needs a full chat page with a sidebar for conversation tabs and a main view for messages, plus all the supporting styles and components.

I'm also building a WebSocket hook to manage the connection, updating the chat context to sync with the backend, adding the chat route to the router, and integrating chat links into the existing navbar and sidebar. The mock data file can be cleaned up once everything's connected.

Actually, let me reconsider the overall component structure by looking at how Telegram organizes its layout. The left sidebar has a search bar at the top, then folder tabs for filtering conversations, followed by the conversation list with avatars, names, last messages, timestamps, and unread indicators. The right side shows the contact info header, the message thread in the middle, and the input area at the bottom. For this recruiting platform, I'd adapt the folder concept into filter tabs instead. So I'm simplifying the structure to have a main Chat page that manages the two-panel layout, with the left panel containing search, tabs, and conversation items, and the right panel handling the message display and input.

I can reuse some existing components like ChatMessage and ChatInput since they're already built, but the sidebar versions are pretty minimal for a 280px view. Rather than trying to make everything responsive, I think it makes more sense to keep the sidebar components as-is and create new, more feature-rich versions for the full page layout. So I'll share ChatMessage and ChatInput across both contexts, but build out new components specifically for the full-page chat experience with enhanced conversation lists, search, and tab filtering.

Now I'm thinking through what the user actually needs here—a separate chat page that works like Telegram. The core features would be a `/chat` route with a two-panel layout: conversations on the left with search and tabs, messages on the right with a header and input area, plus pinning support and WebSocket integration for live updates. Let me map out the specific implementation plan and check what infrastructure already exists.

I'm deciding whether to refactor the existing ChatContext to pull conversations from the API and handle WebSocket messages, or create something new. Since the sidebar mini-chat already uses ChatContext, refactoring it to fetch from the API while keeping the same interface makes sense—both the sidebar and full page can share it. The main technical challenge is setting up WebSocket properly with Hono and the Node server adapter.

Setting up WebSocket handlers...

For the auto-reply simulation, the server receives a message over WebSocket, stores it, broadcasts it to all connections, then after a delay generates and broadcasts a contextual response.

Now I need to think through the full implementation plan, but first I should verify the Vite proxy configuration handles WebSocket upgrades properly since the frontend at port 3000 needs to connect to the API at port 3001.

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a Telegram-like chat page in the Rearden project. Here's the full context:

## Current Architecture
- **Monorepo**: Turborepo with apps/web (Vite+React 19, port 3000), apps/api (Hono+Node, port 3001), packages/types, packages/tsconfig
- **Backend**: Hono with @hono/node-server. In-memory data storage (candidates in mockCandidates.ts). Prisma 7 + SQLite configured but NOT used yet. No auth. No WebSocket.
- **Frontend**: React 19, Motion v12 (`motion/react`), SCSS Modules (camelCase), glass morphism design with 10 themes, spring animations everywhere
- **Existing chat**: Sidebar mini-chat using ChatContext (mock data). Components: ChatPanel, ChatMessage, ChatInput, ConversationList — all designed for 280px sidebar
- **Vite proxy**: `/api` proxied to localhost:3001. No WebSocket proxy configured.
- **API entry** (index.ts): `serve({ fetch: app.fetch, port })` from @hono/node-server
- **Types package**: Raw .ts exports, no build

## User Requirements
1. Separate chat PAGE at `/chat` (not just sidebar)
2. Connected to backend with live messages (WebSocket)
3. Pin/unpin chats
4. Chat tabs/folders (like Telegram: All, Unread, Pinned)
5. Telegram-like UX
6. Remilia-style design (glass morphism, spring animations, grain texture — already the project's design language)

## Key Decisions Already Made
1. **In-memory storage on backend** (matching existing candidate pattern) — no Prisma migration needed
2. **@hono/node-ws** for WebSocket support
3. **Simulated auto-replies** from candidates (2-5s delay) to demonstrate live messaging
4. **Shared ChatContext** serves both sidebar mini-chat and full page
5. Sidebar mini-chat stays but links to full page for expanded view

## Constraints
- Must pass `npm run check-types` and `npm run build`
- Follow existing patterns: SCSS Modules with camelCase, Motion v12 springs, @rearden/types for shared types
- No auth system (hardcode recruiter identity)
- Motion imports: `from "motion/react"` NOT `"framer-motion"`
- Prisma 7: uses `prisma.config.ts` with `defineConfig()`, NOT url in schema.prisma

## Design the plan covering:

### Backend
- New dependency: @hono/node-ws
- In-memory chat store (chatStore.ts): conversations + messages with CRUD operations
- REST routes: GET/POST conversations, POST messages, PATCH pin/unpin
- WebSocket handler: live messaging, typing indicators, auto-reply simulation
- Update app.ts to mount chat routes
- Update index.ts to inject WebSocket

### Types (packages/types)  
- New chat.ts with: Conversation, Message, ChatTab, WSEvent types
- Export from index.ts

### Frontend
- New Chat page with two-panel Telegram layout:
  - Left panel (~340px): search, tab bar (All/Unread/Pinned), conversation list with pin icons
  - Right panel: header with name + pin button, messages area, input area
  - On mobile: single panel with navigation between list and conversation
- useWebSocket hook for live connection
- Refactor ChatContext to connect to backend API + WebSocket instead of mock data
- Vite proxy update for WebSocket (`/ws` → localhost:3001)
- Add `/chat` route and nav link
- Sidebar: keep mini-chat but add "Open Chat" link to full page

### Provide:
1. Complete file list (new + modified) with brief descriptions
2. Implementation order (what depends on what)
3. The WebSocket message protocol
4. Component hierarchy for the chat page

content.content:
1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code when working with code in this repository.
     4→
     5→## Architecture
     6→
     7→**Rearden** is a video-first hiring platform built as a Turborepo monorepo.
     8→
     9→### Workspace Structure
    10→
    11→- **apps/** - Applications
    12→  - `web` - Vite + React 19 frontend (port 3000)
    13→  - `api` - Hono + Node.js backend (port 3001)
    14→
    15→- **packages/** - Shared packages
    16→  - `@rearden/types` - Shared TypeScript types (raw .ts, no build step)
    17→  - `@rearden/tsconfig` - Shared TypeScript configurations
    18→
    19→### Key Patterns
    20→
    21→- **Scope**: All packages use `@rearden/*` namespace
    22→- **Types**: Import from `@rearden/types` — exported as raw `.ts` source
    23→- **Styling**: SCSS Modules with camelCase-only class names
    24→- **Animation**: Motion v12 (`import { motion } from "motion/react"`)
    25→- **API**: Hono with typed `ApiResponse<T>` wrapper
    26→- **Database**: Prisma 7 with SQLite (dev), generated client in `src/generated/prisma`
    27→- **API runner**: `tsx` with watch mode for development
    28→
    29→## Development Commands
    30→
    31→```bash
    32→# Run all apps in parallel
    33→npm run dev
    34→
    35→# Run specific app only
    36→npm run dev -- --filter=web
    37→npm run dev -- --filter=api
    38→
    39→# Build all
    40→npm run build
    41→
    42→# Type check all workspaces
    43→npm run check-types
    44→
    45→# Format code
    46→npm run format
    47→```
    48→
    49→## Important Details
    50→
    51→- **Package Manager**: npm (v11.6.2+)
    52→- **Node Version**: >=18
    53→- **TypeScript**: v5.9.2 across all workspaces
    54→- **Vite**: v6.x with @vitejs/plugin-react
    55→- **Hono**: v4.x with @hono/node-server
    56→- **Prisma**: v7.x with SQLite
    57→- **React**: v19 with Motion v12
    58→
    59→## Turborepo Filters
    60→
    61→- `--filter=web` - Target the web app
    62→- `--filter=api` - Target the API app
    63→- `--filter=@rearden/types` - Target the types package
    64→
    65→## Workspace Dependencies
    66→
    67→Both `web` and `api` depend on `@rearden/types` via workspace protocol (`"@rearden/types": "*"`).
    68→

data.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a Telegram-like chat page in the Rearden project. Here's the full context:

## Current Architecture
- **Monorepo**: Turborepo with apps/web (Vite+React 19, port 3000), apps/api (Hono+Node, port 3001), packages/types, packages/tsconfig
- **Backend**: Hono with @hono/node-server. In-memory data storage (candidates in mockCandidates.ts). Prisma 7 + SQLite configured but NOT used yet. No auth. No WebSocket.
- **Frontend**: React 19, Motion v12 (`motion/react`), SCSS Modules (camelCase), glass morphism design with 10 themes, spring animations everywhere
- **Existing chat**: Sidebar mini-chat using ChatContext (mock data). Components: ChatPanel, ChatMessage, ChatInput, ConversationList — all designed for 280px sidebar
- **Vite proxy**: `/api` proxied to localhost:3001. No WebSocket proxy configured.
- **API entry** (index.ts): `serve({ fetch: app.fetch, port })` from @hono/node-server
- **Types package**: Raw .ts exports, no build

## User Requirements
1. Separate chat PAGE at `/chat` (not just sidebar)
2. Connected to backend with live messages (WebSocket)
3. Pin/unpin chats
4. Chat tabs/folders (like Telegram: All, Unread, Pinned)
5. Telegram-like UX
6. Remilia-style design (glass morphism, spring animations, grain texture — already the project's design language)

## Key Decisions Already Made
1. **In-memory storage on backend** (matching existing candidate pattern) — no Prisma migration needed
2. **@hono/node-ws** for WebSocket support
3. **Simulated auto-replies** from candidates (2-5s delay) to demonstrate live messaging
4. **Shared ChatContext** serves both sidebar mini-chat and full page
5. Sidebar mini-chat stays but links to full page for expanded view

## Constraints
- Must pass `npm run check-types` and `npm run build`
- Follow existing patterns: SCSS Modules with camelCase, Motion v12 springs, @rearden/types for shared types
- No auth system (hardcode recruiter identity)
- Motion imports: `from "motion/react"` NOT `"framer-motion"`
- Prisma 7: uses `prisma.config.ts` with `defineConfig()`, NOT url in schema.prisma

## Design the plan covering:

### Backend
- New dependency: @hono/node-ws
- In-memory chat store (chatStore.ts): conversations + messages with CRUD operations
- REST routes: GET/POST conversations, POST messages, PATCH pin/unpin
- WebSocket handler: live messaging, typing indicators, auto-reply simulation
- Update app.ts to mount chat routes
- Update index.ts to inject WebSocket

### Types (packages/types)  
- New chat.ts with: Conversation, Message, ChatTab, WSEvent types
- Export from index.ts

### Frontend
- New Chat page with two-panel Telegram layout:
  - Left panel (~340px): search, tab bar (All/Unread/Pinned), conversation list with pin icons
  - Right panel: header with name + pin button, messages area, input area
  - On mobile: single panel with navigation between list and conversation
- useWebSocket hook for live connection
- Refactor ChatContext to connect to backend API + WebSocket instead of mock data
- Vite proxy update for WebSocket (`/ws` → localhost:3001)
- Add `/chat` route and nav link
- Sidebar: keep mini-chat but add "Open Chat" link to full page

### Provide:
1. Complete file list (new + modified) with brief descriptions
2. Implementation order (what depends on what)
3. The WebSocket message protocol
4. Component hierarchy for the chat page

data.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a Telegram-like chat page in the Rearden project. Here's the full context:

## Current Architecture
- **Monorepo**: Turborepo with apps/web (Vite+React 19, port 3000), apps/api (Hono+Node, port 3001), packages/types, packages/tsconfig
- **Backend**: Hono with @hono/node-server. In-memory data storage (candidates in mockCandidates.ts). Prisma 7 + SQLite configured but NOT used yet. No auth. No WebSocket.
- **Frontend**: React 19, Motion v12 (`motion/react`), SCSS Modules (camelCase), glass morphism design with 10 themes, spring animations everywhere
- **Existing chat**: Sidebar mini-chat using ChatContext (mock data). Components: ChatPanel, ChatMessage, ChatInput, ConversationList — all designed for 280px sidebar
- **Vite proxy**: `/api` proxied to localhost:3001. No WebSocket proxy configured.
- **API entry** (index.ts): `serve({ fetch: app.fetch, port })` from @hono/node-server
- **Types package**: Raw .ts exports, no build

## User Requirements
1. Separate chat PAGE at `/chat` (not just sidebar)
2. Connected to backend with live messages (WebSocket)
3. Pin/unpin chats
4. Chat tabs/folders (like Telegram: All, Unread, Pinned)
5. Telegram-like UX
6. Remilia-style design (glass morphism, spring animations, grain texture — already the project's design language)

## Key Decisions Already Made
1. **In-memory storage on backend** (matching existing candidate pattern) — no Prisma migration needed
2. **@hono/node-ws** for WebSocket support
3. **Simulated auto-replies** from candidates (2-5s delay) to demonstrate live messaging
4. **Shared ChatContext** serves both sidebar mini-chat and full page
5. Sidebar mini-chat stays but links to full page for expanded view

## Constraints
- Must pass `npm run check-types` and `npm run build`
- Follow existing patterns: SCSS Modules with camelCase, Motion v12 springs, @rearden/types for shared types
- No auth system (hardcode recruiter identity)
- Motion imports: `from "motion/react"` NOT `"framer-motion"`
- Prisma 7: uses `prisma.config.ts` with `defineConfig()`, NOT url in schema.prisma

## Design the plan covering:

### Backend
- New dependency: @hono/node-ws
- In-memory chat store (chatStore.ts): conversations + messages with CRUD operations
- REST routes: GET/POST conversations, POST messages, PATCH pin/unpin
- WebSocket handler: live messaging, typing indicators, auto-reply simulation
- Update app.ts to mount chat routes
- Update index.ts to inject WebSocket

### Types (packages/types)  
- New chat.ts with: Conversation, Message, ChatTab, WSEvent types
- Export from index.ts

### Frontend
- New Chat page with two-panel Telegram layout:
  - Left panel (~340px): search, tab bar (All/Unread/Pinned), conversation list with pin icons
  - Right panel: header with name + pin button, messages area, input area
  - On mobile: single panel with navigation between list and conversation
- useWebSocket hook for live connection
- Refactor ChatContext to connect to backend API + WebSocket instead of mock data
- Vite proxy update for WebSocket (`/ws` → localhost:3001)
- Add `/chat` route and nav link
- Sidebar: keep mini-chat but add "Open Chat" link to full page

### Provide:
1. Complete file list (new + modified) with brief descriptions
2. Implementation order (what depends on what)
3. The WebSocket message protocol
4. Component hierarchy for the chat page

data.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a Telegram-like chat page in the Rearden project. Here's the full context:

## Current Architecture
- **Monorepo**: Turborepo with apps/web (Vite+React 19, port 3000), apps/api (Hono+Node, port 3001), packages/types, packages/tsconfig
- **Backend**: Hono with @hono/node-server. In-memory data storage (candidates in mockCandidates.ts). Prisma 7 + SQLite configured but NOT used yet. No auth. No WebSocket.
- **Frontend**: React 19, Motion v12 (`motion/react`), SCSS Modules (camelCase), glass morphism design with 10 themes, spring animations everywhere
- **Existing chat**: Sidebar mini-chat using ChatContext (mock data). Components: ChatPanel, ChatMessage, ChatInput, ConversationList — all designed for 280px sidebar
- **Vite proxy**: `/api` proxied to localhost:3001. No WebSocket proxy configured.
- **API entry** (index.ts): `serve({ fetch: app.fetch, port })` from @hono/node-server
- **Types package**: Raw .ts exports, no build

## User Requirements
1. Separate chat PAGE at `/chat` (not just sidebar)
2. Connected to backend with live messages (WebSocket)
3. Pin/unpin chats
4. Chat tabs/folders (like Telegram: All, Unread, Pinned)
5. Telegram-like UX
6. Remilia-style design (glass morphism, spring animations, grain texture — already the project's design language)

## Key Decisions Already Made
1. **In-memory storage on backend** (matching existing candidate pattern) — no Prisma migration needed
2. **@hono/node-ws** for WebSocket support
3. **Simulated auto-replies** from candidates (2-5s delay) to demonstrate live messaging
4. **Shared ChatContext** serves both sidebar mini-chat and full page
5. Sidebar mini-chat stays but links to full page for expanded view

## Constraints
- Must pass `npm run check-types` and `npm run build`
- Follow existing patterns: SCSS Modules with camelCase, Motion v12 springs, @rearden/types for shared types
- No auth system (hardcode recruiter identity)
- Motion imports: `from "motion/react"` NOT `"framer-motion"`
- Prisma 7: uses `prisma.config.ts` with `defineConfig()`, NOT url in schema.prisma

## Design the plan covering:

### Backend
- New dependency: @hono/node-ws
- In-memory chat store (chatStore.ts): conversations + messages with CRUD operations
- REST routes: GET/POST conversations, POST messages, PATCH pin/unpin
- WebSocket handler: live messaging, typing indicators, auto-reply simulation
- Update app.ts to mount chat routes
- Update index.ts to inject WebSocket

### Types (packages/types)  
- New chat.ts with: Conversation, Message, ChatTab, WSEvent types
- Export from index.ts

### Frontend
- New Chat page with two-panel Telegram layout:
  - Left panel (~340px): search, tab bar (All/Unread/Pinned), conversation list with pin icons
  - Right panel: header with name + pin button, messages area, input area
  - On mobile: single panel with navigation between list and conversation
- useWebSocket hook for live connection
- Refactor ChatContext to connect to backend API + WebSocket instead of mock data
- Vite proxy update for WebSocket (`/ws` → localhost:3001)
- Add `/chat` route and nav link
- Sidebar: keep mini-chat but add "Open Chat" link to full page

### Provide:
1. Complete file list (new + modified) with brief descriptions
2. Implementation order (what depends on what)
3. The WebSocket message protocol
4. Component hierarchy for the chat page

data.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a Telegram-like chat page in the Rearden project. Here's the full context:

## Current Architecture
- **Monorepo**: Turborepo with apps/web (Vite+React 19, port 3000), apps/api (Hono+Node, port 3001), packages/types, packages/tsconfig
- **Backend**: Hono with @hono/node-server. In-memory data storage (candidates in mockCandidates.ts). Prisma 7 + SQLite configured but NOT used yet. No auth. No WebSocket.
- **Frontend**: React 19, Motion v12 (`motion/react`), SCSS Modules (camelCase), glass morphism design with 10 themes, spring animations everywhere
- **Existing chat**: Sidebar mini-chat using ChatContext (mock data). Components: ChatPanel, ChatMessage, ChatInput, ConversationList — all designed for 280px sidebar
- **Vite proxy**: `/api` proxied to localhost:3001. No WebSocket proxy configured.
- **API entry** (index.ts): `serve({ fetch: app.fetch, port })` from @hono/node-server
- **Types package**: Raw .ts exports, no build

## User Requirements
1. Separate chat PAGE at `/chat` (not just sidebar)
2. Connected to backend with live messages (WebSocket)
3. Pin/unpin chats
4. Chat tabs/folders (like Telegram: All, Unread, Pinned)
5. Telegram-like UX
6. Remilia-style design (glass morphism, spring animations, grain texture — already the project's design language)

## Key Decisions Already Made
1. **In-memory storage on backend** (matching existing candidate pattern) — no Prisma migration needed
2. **@hono/node-ws** for WebSocket support
3. **Simulated auto-replies** from candidates (2-5s delay) to demonstrate live messaging
4. **Shared ChatContext** serves both sidebar mini-chat and full page
5. Sidebar mini-chat stays but links to full page for expanded view

## Constraints
- Must pass `npm run check-types` and `npm run build`
- Follow existing patterns: SCSS Modules with camelCase, Motion v12 springs, @rearden/types for shared types
- No auth system (hardcode recruiter identity)
- Motion imports: `from "motion/react"` NOT `"framer-motion"`
- Prisma 7: uses `prisma.config.ts` with `defineConfig()`, NOT url in schema.prisma

## Design the plan covering:

### Backend
- New dependency: @hono/node-ws
- In-memory chat store (chatStore.ts): conversations + messages with CRUD operations
- REST routes: GET/POST conversations, POST messages, PATCH pin/unpin
- WebSocket handler: live messaging, typing indicators, auto-reply simulation
- Update app.ts to mount chat routes
- Update index.ts to inject WebSocket

### Types (packages/types)  
- New chat.ts with: Conversation, Message, ChatTab, WSEvent types
- Export from index.ts

### Frontend
- New Chat page with two-panel Telegram layout:
  - Left panel (~340px): search, tab bar (All/Unread/Pinned), conversation list with pin icons
  - Right panel: header with name + pin button, messages area, input area
  - On mobile: single panel with navigation between list and conversation
- useWebSocket hook for live connection
- Refactor ChatContext to connect to backend API + WebSocket instead of mock data
- Vite proxy update for WebSocket (`/ws` → localhost:3001)
- Add `/chat` route and nav link
- Sidebar: keep mini-chat but add "Open Chat" link to full page

### Provide:
1. Complete file list (new + modified) with brief descriptions
2. Implementation order (what depends on what)
3. The WebSocket message protocol
4. Component hierarchy for the chat page

- **Frontend**: React 19, Motion v12 (`motion/react`), SCSS Modules (camelCase), glass morphism design with 10 themes, spring animations everywhere
- **Existing chat**: Sidebar mini-chat using ChatContext (mock data). Components: ChatPanel, ChatMessage, ChatInput, ConversationList — all designed for 280px sidebar
- **Vite proxy**: `/api` proxied to localhost:3001. No WebSocket proxy configured.
- **API entry** (index.ts): `serve({ fetch: app.fetch, port })` from @hono/node-server
- **Types package**: Raw .ts exports, no build

## User Requirements
1. Separate chat PAGE at `/chat` (not just sidebar)
2. Connected to backend with live messages (WebSocket)
3. Pin/unpin chats
4. Chat tabs/folders (like Telegram: All, Unread, Pinned)
5. Telegram-like UX
6. Remilia-style design (glass morphism, spring animations, grain texture — already the project's design language)

## Key Decisions Already Made
1. **In-memory storage on backend** (matching existing candidate pattern) — no Prisma migration needed
2. **@hono/node-ws** for WebSocket support
3. **Simulated auto-replies** from candidates (2-5s delay) to demonstrate live messaging
4. **Shared ChatContext** serves both sidebar mini-chat and full page
5. Sidebar mini-chat stays but links to full page for expanded view

## Constraints
- Must pass `npm run check-types` and `npm run build`
- Follow existing patterns: SCSS Modules with camelCase, Motion v12 springs, @rearden/types for shared types
- No auth system (hardcode recruiter identity)
- Motion imports: `from "motion/react"` NOT `"framer-motion"`
- Prisma 7: uses `prisma.config.ts` with `defineConfig()`, NOT url in schema.prisma

- In-memory chat store (chatStore.ts): conversations + messages with CRUD operations

- New chat.ts with: Conversation, Message, ChatTab, WSEvent types
- Export from index.ts

- Must pass `npm run check-types` and `npm run build`
- Follow existing patterns: SCSS Modules with camelCase, Motion v12 springs, @rearden/types for shared types

- Motion imports: `from "motion/react"` NOT `"framer-motion"`
- Prisma 7: uses `prisma.config.ts` with `defineConfig()`, NOT url in schema.prisma

### Backend
- New dependency: @hono/node-ws
- In-memory chat store (chatStore.ts): conversations + messages with CRUD operations

- WebSocket handler: live messaging, typing indicators, auto-reply simulation
- Update app.ts to mount chat routes
- Update index.ts to inject WebSocket

- New chat.ts with: Conversation, Message, ChatTab, WSEvent types
- Export from index.ts

  - Left panel (~340px): search, tab bar (All/Unread/Pinned), conversation list with pin icons
  - Right panel: header with name + pin button, messages area, input area
  - On mobile: single panel with navigation between list and conversation

- Vite proxy update for WebSocket (`/ws` → localhost:3001)

- **Frontend**: React 19, Motion v12 (`motion/react`), SCSS Modules (camelCase), glass morphism design with 10 themes, spring animations everywhere
- **Existing chat**: Sidebar mini-chat using ChatContext (mock data). Components: ChatPanel, ChatMessage, ChatInput, ConversationList — all designed for 280px sidebar
- **Vite proxy**: `/api` proxied to localhost:3001. No WebSocket proxy configured.
- **API entry** (index.ts): `serve({ fetch: app.fetch, port })` from @hono/node-server
- **Types package**: Raw .ts exports, no build

## User Requirements
1. Separate chat PAGE at `/chat` (not just sidebar)
2. Connected to backend with live messages (WebSocket)
3. Pin/unpin chats
4. Chat tabs/folders (like Telegram: All, Unread, Pinned)
5. Telegram-like UX
6. Remilia-style design (glass morphism, spring animations, grain texture — already the project's design language)

## Key Decisions Already Made
1. **In-memory storage on backend** (matching existing candidate pattern) — no Prisma migration needed
2. **@hono/node-ws** for WebSocket support
3. **Simulated auto-replies** from candidates (2-5s delay) to demonstrate live messaging
4. **Shared ChatContext** serves both sidebar mini-chat and full page
5. Sidebar mini-chat stays but links to full page for expanded view

## Constraints
- Must pass `npm run check-types` and `npm run build`
- Follow existing patterns: SCSS Modules with camelCase, Motion v12 springs, @rearden/types for shared types
- No auth system (hardcode recruiter identity)
- Motion imports: `from "motion/react"` NOT `"framer-motion"`
- Prisma 7: uses `prisma.config.ts` with `defineConfig()`, NOT url in schema.prisma

## Design the plan covering:

### Backend
- New dependency: @hono/node-ws
- In-memory chat store (chatStore.ts): conversations + messages with CRUD operations
- REST routes: GET/POST conversations, POST messages, PATCH pin/unpin
- WebSocket handler: live messaging, typing indicators, auto-reply simulation
- Update app.ts to mount chat routes
- Update index.ts to inject WebSocket

### Types (packages/types)  
- New chat.ts with: Conversation, Message, ChatTab, WSEvent types
- Export from index.ts

### Frontend
- New Chat page with two-panel Telegram layout:
  - Left panel (~340px): search, tab bar (All/Unread/Pinned), conversation list with pin icons
  - Right panel: header with name + pin button, messages area, input area
  - On mobile: single panel with navigation between list and conversation
- useWebSocket hook for live connection
- Refactor ChatContext to connect to backend API + WebSocket instead of mock data
- Vite proxy update for WebSocket (`/ws` → localhost:3001)
- Add `/chat` route and nav link
- Sidebar: keep mini-chat but add "Open Chat" link to full page

### Provide:
1. Complete file list (new + modified) with brief descriptions
2. Implementation order (what depends on what)
3. The WebSocket message protocol
4. Component hierarchy for the chat page

content.text:
### Create Node.js Server Instance Without Starting

Source: https://context7.com/honojs/node-server/llms.txt

Creates a Node.js server instance for a Hono application without automatically starting the listening process. This allows for manual control over socket binding (UNIX domain or TCP) and server lifecycle management. It utilizes 'createAdaptorServer' from '@hono/node-server' and optionally overrides global objects.

```typescript
import { createAdaptorServer } from '@hono/node-server'
import { Hono } from 'hono'

const app = new Hono()
app.get('/', (c) => c.text('Hello World'))

const server = createAdaptorServer({
  fetch: app.fetch,
  overrideGlobalObjects: true
})

// Listen on UNIX domain socket
const socketPath = '/tmp/app.sock'
server.listen(socketPath, () => {
  console.log(`Server listening on ${socketPath}`)
})

// Or listen on TCP port with custom logic
server.listen(8080, '0.0.0.0', () => {
  console.log('Server started on all interfaces')
})
```

--------------------------------

### Listen to a UNIX domain socket

Source: https://github.com/honojs/node-server/blob/main/README.md

Configure the Hono Node.js server to listen on a UNIX domain socket instead of a TCP port using `createAdaptorServer`.

```APIDOC
## Server Configuration

### Description
Configures the Hono Node.js server to listen on a specified UNIX domain socket path.

### Method
N/A (Server configuration)

### Endpoint
N/A (Server configuration)

### Usage
```ts
import { createAdaptorServer } from '@hono/node-server'

// Assuming 'app' is your Hono application instance
const app = new Hono()

const server = createAdaptorServer(app)
const socketPath = '/tmp/example.sock'

server.listen(socketPath, () => {
  console.log(`Listening on ${socketPath}`)
})
```

### Notes
- This replaces the standard `serve(app)` method for TCP listening.
- Ensure the directory for the socket path exists and has appropriate permissions.
```

--------------------------------

### createAdaptorServer - Create Server Without Starting

Source: https://context7.com/honojs/node-server/llms.txt

Creates a Node.js server instance without automatically listening for connections. This allows for manual control over socket binding and server lifecycle management, including listening on UNIX domain sockets or TCP ports.

```APIDOC
## POST /api/create-adaptor-server

### Description
Creates a Node.js server instance for a Hono application without automatically starting it.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/api/create-adaptor-server

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **fetch** (Function) - Required - The fetch handler of the Hono application.
- **overrideGlobalObjects** (Boolean) - Optional - Whether to override global objects like `Request` and `Response`. Defaults to `false`.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "fetch": "app.fetch",
  "overrideGlobalObjects": true
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **serverInstance** (Object) - The created server instance, which can be used to call `.listen()`.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "serverInstance": "[Server Instance Object]"
}
```

**Note:** The returned `serverInstance` is a Node.js `http.Server` or `https.Server` object and can be used to call `.listen()` on a port or socket.
```

### node-server > Summary

Source: https://context7.com/honojs/node-server/llms.txt

Integration patterns for `@hono/node-server` are straightforward. You can wrap your Hono application with the `serve()` function for standard deployments, use `createAdaptorServer()` for custom server configurations like UNIX sockets, and apply `serveStatic()` middleware for efficient file serving. The adapter maintains full compatibility with Hono's extensive middleware ecosystem, including support for authentication, CORS, compression, and logging. For applications needing Node.js-specific features, the `HttpBindings` type provides direct access to native `IncomingMessage` and `ServerResponse` objects. The `RESPONSE_ALREADY_SENT` constant enables gradual migration from legacy Node.js code by allowing mixed response handling approaches within the same application.

--------------------------------

### Node.js Server > Accessing Node.js API

Source: https://github.com/honojs/node-server/blob/main/README.md

When running Hono on Node.js, you can directly access the underlying Node.js `http.IncomingMessage` and `http.ServerResponse` objects via `c.env`. This is particularly useful for scenarios requiring direct interaction with Node.js APIs. The `HttpBindings` type defines the structure of these bindings, providing access to `incoming` and `outgoing` properties. For instance, you can retrieve the client's remote address from `c.env.incoming.socket.remoteAddress`.

Design a detailed implementation plan for a Telegram-like chat page in the Rearden project. Here's the full context:

## Current Architecture
- **Monorepo**: Turborepo with apps/web (Vite+React 19, port 3000), apps/api (Hono+Node, port 3001), packages/types, packages/tsconfig
- **Backend**: Hono with @hono/node-server. In-memory data storage (candidates in mockCandidates.ts). Prisma 7 + SQLite configured but NOT used yet. No auth. No WebSocket.
- **Frontend**: React 19, Motion v12 (`motion/react`), SCSS Modules (camelCase), glass morphism design with 10 themes, spring animations everywhere
- **Existing chat**: Sidebar mini-chat using ChatContext (mock data). Components: ChatPanel, ChatMessage, ChatInput, ConversationList — all designed for 280px sidebar
- **Vite proxy**: `/api` proxied to localhost:3001. No WebSocket proxy configured.
- **API entry** (index.ts): `serve({ fetch: app.fetch, port })` from @hono/node-server
- **Types package**: Raw .ts exports, no build

## User Requirements
1. Separate chat PAGE at `/chat` (not just sidebar)
2. Connected to backend with live messages (WebSocket)
3. Pin/unpin chats
4. Chat tabs/folders (like Telegram: All, Unread, Pinned)
5. Telegram-like UX
6. Remilia-style design (glass morphism, spring animations, grain texture — already the project's design language)

## Key Decisions Already Made
1. **In-memory storage on backend** (matching existing candidate pattern) — no Prisma migration needed
2. **@hono/node-ws** for WebSocket support
3. **Simulated auto-replies** from candidates (2-5s delay) to demonstrate live messaging
4. **Shared ChatContext** serves both sidebar mini-chat and full page
5. Sidebar mini-chat stays but links to full page for expanded view

## Constraints
- Must pass `npm run check-types` and `npm run build`
- Follow existing patterns: SCSS Modules with camelCase, Motion v12 springs, @rearden/types for shared types
- No auth system (hardcode recruiter identity)
- Motion imports: `from "motion/react"` NOT `"framer-motion"`
- Prisma 7: uses `prisma.config.ts` with `defineConfig()`, NOT url in schema.prisma

## Design the plan covering:

### Backend
- New dependency: @hono/node-ws
- In-memory chat store (chatStore.ts): conversations + messages with CRUD operations
- REST routes: GET/POST conversations, POST messages, PATCH pin/unpin
- WebSocket handler: live messaging, typing indicators, auto-reply simulation
- Update app.ts to mount chat routes
- Update index.ts to inject WebSocket

### Types (packages/types)  
- New chat.ts with: Conversation, Message, ChatTab, WSEvent types
- Export from index.ts

### Frontend
- New Chat page with two-panel Telegram layout:
  - Left panel (~340px): search, tab bar (All/Unread/Pinned), conversation list with pin icons
  - Right panel: header with name + pin button, messages area, input area
  - On mobile: single panel with navigation between list and conversation
- useWebSocket hook for live connection
- Refactor ChatContext to connect to backend API + WebSocket instead of mock data
- Vite proxy update for WebSocket (`/ws` → localhost:3001)
- Add `/chat` route and nav link
- Sidebar: keep mini-chat but add "Open Chat" link to full page

### Provide:
1. Complete file list (new + modified) with brief descriptions
2. Implementation order (what depends on what)
3. The WebSocket message protocol
4. Component hierarchy for the chat page

content.text:
## hono/node-ws - NPM https://npmjs.com/package/@hono/node-ws @hono/node-ws - npm # @hono/node-ws 1.3.0 • Public • Published 2 months ago - 19 Versions - 78 Dependents - 1 Dependency - Code Beta - Readme # WebSocket helper for Node.js A WebSocket helper for Node.js ## Usage ``` import { createNodeWebSocket } from '@hono/node-ws' import { Hono } from 'hono' import { serve } from '@hono/node-server' const app = new Hono() const { injectWebSocket, upgradeWebSocket } = createNodeWebSocket({ app }) app.get( '/ws', upgradeWebSocket((c) => ({ // https://hono.dev/helpers/websocket })) ) const server = serve(app) injectWebSocket(server) ``` ## Author Shotaro Nakamura https://github.com/nakasyou ## License MIT ## Readme ### Keywords none Viewing @hono/node-ws version 1.3.0 ## Setup example for web sockets in AWS Lambda #4549 - GitHub https://github.com/honojs/hono/issues/4549 example for web sockets in AWS Lambda - Author: @muezz - State: open - Labels: enhancement - Created: 2025-11-26T12:00:03Z ... I am working on an API where I need to have a websocket route. Since I need to deploy on AWS Lambda, I am looking to ... ```typescript // apps/backend/lambda/index.ts import { serve } from "@hono/node-server"; serve( { fetch: app.fetch, port: 3000, }, (info) => { ... As you can see, `app.ts` holds the server logic, `index.ts` allows me to run the server locally and I use `handler.ts` ... ```typescript // apps/backend/lambda/app.ts import { Hono } from "hono/quick"; import { createNodeWebSocket } from "@hono/node-ws"; const app = new Hono().basePath("/api"); const { injectWebSocket, upgradeWebSocket } = createNodeWebSocket({ app }); app.route("/auth", authRoutes); app.get( "/ws", upgradeWebSocket((c) => ({ // logic for websockets goes here })) ); export default app; ``` I am struggling here as `createNodeWebSocket` comes from `@hono/node-ws` but `app.ts` needs to be runtime agnostic so it can be used in node for local dev on in AWS Lambda for production. I am also not sure how, where or when to call `injectWebSocket`. It would be great if an example could be added in the docs about how to set up websockets in hono in AWS Lambda. --- ### Timeline **muezz** added label `enhancement` · Nov 26, 2025 at 12pm **AyushCoder9** mentioned this in PR [#4550: feat: add AWS Lambda ## WebSocket Helper - Hono https://hono.dev/docs/helpers/websocket { upgradeWebSocket } from 'hono/cloudflare-workers' ``` ... ```ts import { Hono } from 'hono' import { upgradeWebSocket, websocket } from 'hono/bun' //... export default { fetch: app.fetch, websocket, } ``` If you use Node.js, you can use @hono/node-ws. ## `upgradeWebSocket()` `upgradeWebSocket()` returns a handler for handling WebSocket. ```ts const app = new Hono() app.get( '/ws', upgradeWebSocket((c) => { return { onMessage(event, ws) { console.log(`Message from client: ${event.data}`) ws.send('Hello from server!') }, onClose: () => { console.log('Connection closed') }, } }) ) ... ```ts // server.ts const wsApp = app.get( '/ws', upgradeWebSocket((c) => { //... }) ) export type WebSocketApp = typeof wsApp ... ``` ## Examples See the examples using WebSocket Helper. ### Server and Client ```ts // server.ts import { Hono } from 'hono' import { upgradeWebSocket } from 'hono/cloudflare-workers' const app = new Hono().get( '/ws', upgradeWebSocket(() => { return { onMessage: (event) => { ... const client = hc('http://localhost:8787') const ws = client.ws.$ws(0) ... ```tsx import { Hono } from 'hono' import { upgradeWebSocket, websocket } from 'hono/bun' ...

data.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a Telegram-like chat page in the Rearden project. Here's the full context:

## Current Architecture
- **Monorepo**: Turborepo with apps/web (Vite+React 19, port 3000), apps/api (Hono+Node, port 3001), packages/types, packages/tsconfig
- **Backend**: Hono with @hono/node-server. In-memory data storage (candidates in mockCandidates.ts). Prisma 7 + SQLite configured but NOT used yet. No auth. No WebSocket.
- **Frontend**: React 19, Motion v12 (`motion/react`), SCSS Modules (camelCase), glass morphism design with 10 themes, spring animations everywhere
- **Existing chat**: Sidebar mini-chat using ChatContext (mock data). Components: ChatPanel, ChatMessage, ChatInput, ConversationList — all designed for 280px sidebar
- **Vite proxy**: `/api` proxied to localhost:3001. No WebSocket proxy configured.
- **API entry** (index.ts): `serve({ fetch: app.fetch, port })` from @hono/node-server
- **Types package**: Raw .ts exports, no build

## User Requirements
1. Separate chat PAGE at `/chat` (not just sidebar)
2. Connected to backend with live messages (WebSocket)
3. Pin/unpin chats
4. Chat tabs/folders (like Telegram: All, Unread, Pinned)
5. Telegram-like UX
6. Remilia-style design (glass morphism, spring animations, grain texture — already the project's design language)

## Key Decisions Already Made
1. **In-memory storage on backend** (matching existing candidate pattern) — no Prisma migration needed
2. **@hono/node-ws** for WebSocket support
3. **Simulated auto-replies** from candidates (2-5s delay) to demonstrate live messaging
4. **Shared ChatContext** serves both sidebar mini-chat and full page
5. Sidebar mini-chat stays but links to full page for expanded view

## Constraints
- Must pass `npm run check-types` and `npm run build`
- Follow existing patterns: SCSS Modules with camelCase, Motion v12 springs, @rearden/types for shared types
- No auth system (hardcode recruiter identity)
- Motion imports: `from "motion/react"` NOT `"framer-motion"`
- Prisma 7: uses `prisma.config.ts` with `defineConfig()`, NOT url in schema.prisma

## Design the plan covering:

### Backend
- New dependency: @hono/node-ws
- In-memory chat store (chatStore.ts): conversations + messages with CRUD operations
- REST routes: GET/POST conversations, POST messages, PATCH pin/unpin
- WebSocket handler: live messaging, typing indicators, auto-reply simulation
- Update app.ts to mount chat routes
- Update index.ts to inject WebSocket

### Types (packages/types)  
- New chat.ts with: Conversation, Message, ChatTab, WSEvent types
- Export from index.ts

### Frontend
- New Chat page with two-panel Telegram layout:
  - Left panel (~340px): search, tab bar (All/Unread/Pinned), conversation list with pin icons
  - Right panel: header with name + pin button, messages area, input area
  - On mobile: single panel with navigation between list and conversation
- useWebSocket hook for live connection
- Refactor ChatContext to connect to backend API + WebSocket instead of mock data
- Vite proxy update for WebSocket (`/ws` → localhost:3001)
- Add `/chat` route and nav link
- Sidebar: keep mini-chat but add "Open Chat" link to full page

### Provide:
1. Complete file list (new + modified) with brief descriptions
2. Implementation order (what depends on what)
3. The WebSocket message protocol
4. Component hierarchy for the chat page
